name: Archibus IWMS & Space Management API
what_it_does: Archibus REST API for integrated workplace management system with deep CAD and BIM integration. Enables AI agents to manage space and occupancy data retrieval including room assignments, headcount, and department allocations for workplace optimization, handle facility maintenance work request and work order management for operational facilities, access real estate portfolio and lease data for space planning and cost management, retrieve building infrastructure and CAD floor plan data linked to space records for visual workplace management, manage move management and hoteling/desk reservation workflow for hybrid workplace optimization, handle compliance and sustainability reporting data for facilities regulatory management, access strategic capital project planning data for facility improvement programs, retrieve equipment and asset records linked to space for integrated facilities management, manage emergency preparedness and evacuation planning data, and integrate space and facility data with HR, BIM, AutoCAD, and enterprise systems.
use_cases: Optimizing space allocation from workplace management agents
Managing facility maintenance from work order automation agents
Planning office moves from portfolio management agents
Tracking desk reservations from hybrid work management agents
not_for: Field service contractors without enterprise IWMS and space management needs
Industrial plant maintenance without corporate real estate context
Small organizations without enterprise facilities management complexity
best_when: A large corporation, university, or government agency using Archibus wants AI agents to automate space optimization, workplace desk reservations, facilities maintenance, move management, and real estate portfolio analysis.
avoid_when: COMPLIANCE RISK: Archibus space data drives real estate cost allocation and chargeback — automated space assignment changes without finance team review creates incorrect departmental cost allocation. Archibus may contain classified building infrastructure data for government facilities; automated access and data sharing requires security classification review. Lease data changes affecting ASC 842 financial reporting require accounting validation.

auth.notes: Archibus uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. Per-tenant credentials. No native webhooks — integration via Archibus Integration Framework. REST API with JSON. Acquired by Eptura (2022, merger with iOFFICE and SpaceIQ). Boston, Massachusetts origin. Strong in government, higher education, and corporate real estate. Deep AutoCAD/Revit integration via Archibus for AutoCAD product. Competes with Planon, Accruent, and IBM Maximo for global IWMS.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per module, user count, and managed space
pricing.notes: Boston, Massachusetts. Founded 1982. Acquired by Eptura (2022) — Eptura is IWMS/proptech holding company created via merger of iOFFICE and SpaceIQ, then acquired Archibus. 8,000+ clients globally including US federal government (GSA, DoD). Strong in government, universities, and corporate real estate. Deep CAD/BIM integration as key differentiator. Competes with Planon, IBM Maximo, and Accruent.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; client-negotiated

known_agent_gotchas: COMPLIANCE RISK: Archibus space data drives facility cost chargeback calculations; automated space assignment changes without finance sign-off creates incorrect departmental billing
Eptura merger complexity — Archibus was acquired by Eptura (2022) combining with iOFFICE/SpaceIQ; product integration, roadmap, and API stability evolving; verify current product direction
Government FedRAMP footprint — Archibus used by GSA and DoD; government deployments have FedRAMP security requirements and may have classified building data access restrictions
No native webhooks — Archibus integration relies on the Archibus Integration Framework (AIF) rather than native webhooks; real-time event-driven integration requires AIF configuration
CAD/BIM data model complexity — Archibus space data is linked to CAD floor plans; space-level data changes must maintain CAD/BIM model consistency
Legacy on-premise base — Archibus has a large on-premise installed base; cloud migration is ongoing; verify cloud vs. on-premise deployment and API endpoint differences
agent_readiness.security_notes: IWMS and space management. SOC2, FedRAMP, GDPR. OAuth2/API key. US/EU. Government building and space management data.

security_score_components.security_notes: IWMS space management. SOC2, FedRAMP, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Government and corporate space data.
